title: Weather Update: Heatwave Expected Even in Monsoon After Summer, Meteorologists Warn - Lokshahi English News

full_text:
Weather Update: Heatwave Expected Even in Monsoon After Summer, Meteorologists Warn
For the past week, citizens in North and Central India have finally received relief from the heatwave that has been scorching them. The light to moderate rain on Wednesday and Thursday has alleviated the impact of the first heatwave of the season. According to the latest forecast from the Indian Meteorological Department (IMD), the likelihood of another heatwave in the next two weeks is very low. (IMD Weather Update)
However, this relief may be temporary. Meteorologists have warned that this year, not only in summer but also during the monsoon, average temperatures are expected to remain at high levels, which could lead to citizens facing even more severe heatwaves in the coming months. ( Weather Alert)
In most parts of India, summers are always harsh; however, meteorologists have stated that a 'heatwave' is a situation based on specific criteria. If the maximum temperature at a location is at least five degrees Celsius higher than usual and persists for at least two consecutive days, that location is declared to be experiencing a heatwave. If the temperature is seven degrees higher than usual, it is declared a severe heatwave.
Before declaring a heatwave, several other factors are also considered. These include whether the location is in a flat area or a hilly region, as well as whether the normal temperature there is less than or greater than 40°C. However, the main criterion is that when there is an abnormal increase in the temperature of a location, that area is warned of a heatwave. Heatwaves predominantly occur in India between March and June, although sometimes they extend into the monsoon season. In 2023, a heatwave-like situation arose in some parts of the country as early as February. However, there are no provisions in the regulations to declare a heatwave in February.
Summary
- Possibility of a heatwave even during the monsoon.
- IMD states that the chances of a heatwave in the next two weeks are low.
- Abnormal temperatures signal citizens to stay alert.
- The intensity of heat depends on various criteria.

title: Heatwave advisory: No reason for panic, says Jitendra Singh - newsage.in
search_term: (heatwave OR "heat wave" OR "severe heatwave" OR "extreme heat" OR "heat spell" OR "scorching heat" OR "blistering heat" OR "sweltering heat" OR "hot winds" OR loo OR "hot loo winds" OR "intense heat" OR "unbearable heat" OR "deadly heat" OR heat OR "weather forecast" OR "weather update" OR "weather alert" OR "IMD forecast" OR "IMD prediction" OR "IMD alert" OR IMD OR "rising temperatures" OR "temperatures expected to rise" OR "relief from heat" OR "respite from heat" OR "heat respite" OR "respite from scorching heat") Andaman and Nicobar Islands
full_text:
Hyderabad: Union Minister Dr. Jitendra Singh issued a heatwave advisory, saying there was no reason for panic over expected May heat conditions and urging people to follow basic precautions.
He said forecasts indicated above-normal temperatures in some regions, while many areas would see normal or below-normal conditions. Therefore, he stressed that impacts could be managed with preparedness and awareness.
The Minister cited India Meteorological Department projections, which showed likely heatwave conditions in parts of the Himalayan foothills, east coast, Gujarat and Maharashtra. However, he clarified that such conditions would not be uniform nationwide.
He added that higher temperatures were expected during the second and fourth weeks of May. Meanwhile, rainfall and cloud cover could moderate temperatures during the first and third weeks.
He said the government had strengthened coordination with states to ensure water supply, cooling arrangements and uninterrupted electricity. Special focus remains on vulnerable groups such as children, elderly people and outdoor workers.
IMD forecast shapes heatwave advisory response
Dr. Jitendra Singh advised citizens to stay hydrated, avoid outdoor exposure during peak hours and monitor official updates. He said improved forecasting systems now enable timely alerts and better response.
He also addressed agricultural concerns, noting that conditions remained favourable for harvesting late Rabi crops in northern regions. However, he cautioned that localised heat stress could affect some summer crops in southern and northeastern areas.
Farmers were advised to work during cooler hours and ensure proper irrigation and livestock care. He added that ENSO-neutral conditions currently prevail, with El Niño likely to develop during the monsoon.
The Minister reiterated that accurate interpretation of forecasts and adherence to guidelines would help minimise health risks. He urged the public to rely only on official sources and avoid unnecessary alarm.

title: Two deaths due to heat as of May — MOH - The Edge Malaysia
search_term: (heatstroke OR "heat stroke" OR sunstroke OR "heat exhaustion" OR "heat-related death" OR "heat casualty" OR "heat fatality" OR "suspected heat death" OR dehydration OR "heat cramps" OR "heat illness") Andaman and Nicobar Islands
full_text:
PUTRAJAYA (May 3): The Ministry of Health (MOH) has recorded 56 cases related to hot weather nationwide, including two deaths from heat stroke, from Jan 1 to Sunday.
MOH in a statement on Sunday announced that the two deaths involved a two-year-old boy who was left in a vehicle and a 42-year-old man who participated in a marathon in Penang.
"Both cases occurred when weather conditions were below Warning Level 1.
"However, the main risk factors identified were exposure to extreme heat in an indoor environment and strenuous physical activity over a long period of time, which caused severe dehydration and failure to regulate body temperature," the statement said.
According to the statement, there were 47 cases involved heat exhaustion, four cases of exertional heat stroke, four cases of heat stroke and one case of heat cramps.
MOH said analysis showed that 58% of cases of heat-related illnesses were related to physical activity during hot weather.
"Of the total, almost half involved sports and athletic activities such as cross-country, sports training, and marathons, while the rest involved high-intensity physical training by security and operations teams.
"In this regard, MOH would like to advise the public to always be careful and take preventive measures during hot weather," the statement said.
Among the preventive measures suggested by MOH are to limit strenuous physical activity and prolonged exposure to hot weather, and for those working under hot weather to spend more time resting in the shade to cool down.
In addition, the public is also reminded to drink plenty of plain water even if they do not feel thirsty to ensure that the body remains hydrated, as well as to use personal protection such as hats, umbrellas, light and brightly coloured clothing.
Risk groups such as infants, children, the elderly, and individuals with chronic diseases should always be adequately hydrated, and parents and guardians are reminded to avoid leaving children alone in vehicles.
The public is reminded to seek immediate treatment at a health facility if they experience symptoms such as dizziness, nausea, muscle cramps, or extreme fatigue.
"MOH also urges all parties including government agencies, educational institutions and care centres to take appropriate preventive measures, especially in planning and implementing outdoor activities to reduce the risk of heat-related illnesses.
"The public can get the latest information on the status of hot weather on the METMalaysia website and preventive measures to reduce the health effects of hot weather on the MOH website," the statement said.

title: गर्मी और लू से बचाव को लेकर रामगढ़ जिला प्रशासन ने जारी की एडवाइजरी - Prabhat Khabar

full_text:
गर्मी और लू से बचाव को लेकर रामगढ़ जिला प्रशासन ने जारी की एडवाइजरी
एडवाइजरी
Ramgarh News: रामगढ़ में बढ़ती गर्मी और लू को देखते हुए जिला प्रशासन ने एडवाइजरी जारी कर लोगों को सतर्क रहने की अपील की है. इससे जुड़ी खबर नीचे पढ़ें.
रामगढ़ से भागीरथ महतो की रिपोर्ट
Ramgarh News: झारखंड के रामगढ़ जिले में भीषण गर्मी और लू के बढ़ते प्रभाव को देखते हुए जिला प्रशासन और स्वास्थ्य विभाग ने आम नागरिकों के लिए जरूरी सावधानियां और दिशा-निर्देश जारी किए हैं. प्रशासन ने लोगों से अपनी सेहत का विशेष ध्यान रखने की अपील की है.
पर्याप्त पानी और तरल पदार्थ लेने की सलाह
जारी निर्देश में कहा गया है कि नागरिक अधिक से अधिक पानी और तरल पदार्थ जैसे नींबू पानी, छाछ और फलों का जूस का सेवन करें. शरीर को ढक कर रखें और हल्के रंग के ढीले कपड़े पहनें.
धूप से बचाव के लिए जरूरी उपाय
तेज धूप में बाहर निकलते समय छाता, टोपी या गमछे का उपयोग करने की सलाह दी गई है. घरों में खिड़की-दरवाजों पर पर्दे लगाकर धूप को अंदर आने से रोकने को कहा गया है. बाहर निकलते समय जूते-चप्पल पहनना भी जरूरी बताया गया है.
क्या न करें
प्रशासन ने बिना सिर ढके धूप में बाहर न निकलने, अत्यधिक गर्मी में भारी काम या व्यायाम से बचने की सलाह दी है. साथ ही धूप में नंगे पैर चलने और बंद गाड़ियों में बच्चों या पालतू जानवरों को अकेला छोड़ने से मना किया गया है.
खानपान को लेकर भी सतर्कता जरूरी
अधिकारियों ने शराब, चाय, कॉफी और अधिक मीठे या गैस वाले पेय पदार्थों के सेवन से बचने की सलाह दी है. गर्मी के समय हल्का और संतुलित आहार लेने पर जोर दिया गया है.
लक्षण दिखने पर तुरंत करें संपर्क
प्रशासन ने कहा है कि यदि किसी व्यक्ति में चक्कर आना, तेज बुखार या कमजोरी जैसे लक्षण दिखें तो तुरंत नजदीकी स्वास्थ्य केंद्र से संपर्क करें. आपात स्थिति में 108 एंबुलेंस सेवा और 104 हेल्पलाइन का उपयोग करने की अपील की गई है.

प्रभात खबर डिजिटल टॉप स्टोरी
श्वेता वैद्य प्रभात खबर में लाइफस्टाइल बीट के लिए कंटेंट लिखती हैं. वह पिछले एक साल से व्यंजन (Recipes), फैशन, ब्यूटी टिप्स, होम डेकोर, किचन टिप्स और लेटेस्ट मेहंदी डिजाइन्स जैसे विषयों पर लेख लिख रही हैं. उनका उद्देश्य पाठकों को रोजमर्रा की जिंदगी को आसान और स्टाइलिश बनाने के लिए प्रैक्टिकल टिप्स देना है.
Prabhat Khabar App :
देश, एजुकेशन, मनोरंजन, बिजनेस अपडेट, धर्म, क्रिकेट, राशिफल की ताजा खबरें पढ़ें यहां. रोजाना की ब्रेकिंग हिंदी न्यूज और लाइव न्यूज कवरेज के लिए डाउनलोड करिए

गर्मी का कहर : लाखों पौधे सूख रहे, जिम्मेदार बेफिक्र
संवाद न्यूज एजेंसी, चित्रकूट
विज्ञापन
विज्ञापन
चित्रकूट। गर्मी के बढ़ते प्रकोप ने पिछले बरसात में लगाए गए लाखों पौधों के अस्तित्व पर संकट खड़ा कर दिया है। सरकारी कार्यालयों, सड़कों के किनारे और सार्वजनिक स्थानों पर रोपे गए हजारों पौधे सिंचाई व रखरखाव के अभाव में दम तोड़ रहे हैं। अत्यधिक तापमान और लू के थपेड़ों ने स्थिति को गंभीर बना दिया है।
पिछले बरसात के दौरान चित्रकूट जिले में बड़े पैमाने पर वृक्षारोपण अभियान चलाया गया था। इसका उद्देश्य जिले को हरा-भरा बनाना और पर्यावरण संरक्षण को बढ़ावा देना था। राष्ट्रीय राजमार्ग, मिशन सड़क, कसहाई सड़क सहित कई प्रमुख सड़कों के किनारे पौधे लगाए गए थे। इन पौधों की सुरक्षा के लिए जालियां भी लगाई गई थीं। हालांकि, बरसात के बाद इनके रखरखाव और नियमित सिंचाई पर ध्यान नहीं दिया गया। लोक निर्माण विभाग और सिंचाई विभाग कार्यालयों के पौधे भी गर्मी का शिकार हुए हैं। कई जगहों पर पौधे पूरी तरह सूख चुके हैं।
जिम्मेदारों की प्रतिक्रिया
किसान सभा के प्रदेश उपाध्यक्ष रुद्र प्रसाद मिश्र ने वृक्षारोपण में आंकड़ों की बाजीगरी पर चिंता जताई। उन्होंने कहा कि लाखों पौधे लगाने का रिकॉर्ड तो बनता है, पर देखभाल नहीं होती। सपा के पूर्व जिलाध्यक्ष अनुज यादव ने भी सिंचाई व रखरखाव पर आवाज उठाने की बात कही। अपर जिलाधिकारी चंद्रशेखर ने विभागीय अधिकारियों को पौधों की तत्काल सिंचाई कराने के निर्देश दिए हैं।
